channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Sorry – Netflix says it's actually not integrating with the Apple TV app
item.description: After Netflix started appearing in the Apple TV app for some users, the streamer says it was an inadvertent rollout and likely will never see a wide release.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Just 32 percent in US trust AI: Study
item.description: Only about a third of Americans trust artificial intelligence (AI), according to new data from the Edelman Trust Barometer. Just 32 percent of people in the U.S. said they trust the rapidly developing technology, according to the annual global survey’s “Insights for the Technology Sector.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk’s war on USAID is a war on reality
item.description: On January 29, President Donald Trump celebrated the latest victory in unelected billionaire Elon Musk’s crusade against “inefficiency” in the government: stopping $50 million from, according to Trump, “being sent to Gaza to buy condoms for Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: Everything you need to know about Elon Musk’s DOGE staffers
item.description: Though only a few weeks old, already the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) is radically changing the face of bureaucracy in D.C.
item.dc:creator: Lily McDonald, Fast Company

item.title: Severance is officially Apple TV Plus' biggest hit for years – but it’s still miles behind Netflix’s Squid Game
item.description: Apple's streaming service has a lot to celebrate after the latest Neilson viewing stats, with Severance posting the best numbers since a certain Ted Lasso.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Unlocking AI’s full potential in procurement with smarter data management
item.description: Supply chain operations are often stymied by inefficiencies that result in higher costs, longer lead times, and dissatisfied customers.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: UK's Apple iCloud backdoor "jeopardizes the security and privacy of millions," warn experts
item.description: UK's Apple iCloud backdoor "jeopardizes the security and privacy of millions," warn experts. Here's what's at stake.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Apple’s emotional lamp and the future of robots
item.description: Pixar Animation Studios has an unusual logo. The basic logo is the word “Pixar.” But sometimes, an animated lamp named Luxo Jr. hops into the frame and jumps on the letter “i.” The lap exudes personality and represents Pixar’s ability to turn any object into a compelling character.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The Memo: Musk rivals Trump as most controversial figure on political stage
item.description: President Trump may be only the second most controversial person in his own administration. Three weeks into Trump’s second term, Elon Musk has taken center stage — as big of a villain for liberals and Trump critics as he is a hero for MAGA conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, The Hill

item.title: House Commerce chair launches data privacy working group
item.description: House Committee on Energy and Commerce Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) on Wednesday announced the creation of a data privacy working group as policymakers work toward a national privacy standard.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The cloud giants stumble
item.description: Recent financial results from the major cloud providers reveal a concerning trend in cloud adoption and growth. AWS reported just 13% growth in the final quarter of 2023, Microsoft Azure saw 19% growth, and Google Cloud achieved 26% growth—all below market expectations.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FPGAs lose luster in genAI era
item.description: In a world gone mad over GPUs for AI acceleration, FPGA processors are seeing limited adoption in the data center for genAI workloads, with their primary use still in embedded markets and edge AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google’s generative AI Toolbox for Databases to help connect agents with databases
item.description: Google’s cloud computing division, Google Cloud, has added a new generative AI Toolbox for Databases to help enterprise developers connect agent-based generative AI applications to its databases.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Top 20 apps tracking you every day
item.description: The apps collecting the most data about you are among the most widely used. Kurt the CyberGuy takes a look at 20 of these apps and how you can protect personal information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KRON4
